Class Controller

java.lang.Object
sudoku.cli.Controller
All Implemented Interfaces:
IController
public class Controller extends Object implements IController
Concrete implementation of IController, facilitating communication between the Model and View layers of the stack.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Controller(ISudokuGenerator.PuzzleDifficulty difficulty)
    Constructs a controller and immediately starts a new game at the given difficulty.

  • Constructor Details

    • Controller

      public Controller(ISudokuGenerator.PuzzleDifficulty difficulty)
      Constructs a controller and immediately starts a new game at the given difficulty.
      Parameters:
      difficulty - The difficulty, which determines the number of givens in the generated grid.

  • Method Details

    • newGame

      public void newGame(ISudokuGenerator.PuzzleDifficulty difficulty)
      Description copied from interface: IController
      Generates a new sudoku grid, starting with the given difficulty.
      Specified by:
      newGame in interface IController
      Parameters:
      difficulty - The difficulty, which determines the number of givens in the grid.
      See Also:
      • ISudokuGrid
      • ISudokuGenerator.PuzzleDifficulty

    • placeValue

      public void placeValue(int row, int col, int value) throws InvalidCellException, InvalidValueException, GivenCellException
      Description copied from interface: IController
      Attempts to place a given value in the target cell.

      The value to be placed within the cell must fall within the bounds of the size of the ISudokuGrid. For example, a typical grid has a size of 9, meaning the values 1-9 inclusive must be placed exactly once in each row, column, and box.

      Specified by:
      placeValue in interface IController
      Parameters:
      row - The row (or y-coordinate) of the target cell.
      col - The column (or x-coordinate) of the target cell.
      value - The value to attempt to place within the given cell.
      Throws:
      InvalidCellException - if the given cell coordinates do not exist within the Sudoku grid.
      InvalidValueException - if the given value exceeds the size of the Sudoku grid.
      GivenCellException - if the given cell stores a given value: these cannot be overwritten.
      See Also:
      • ISudokuGrid

    • clearCell

      public void clearCell(int row, int col) throws InvalidCellException, EmptyCellException, GivenCellException
      Description copied from interface: IController
      Clears the target cell of the value which it currently stores.
      Specified by:
      clearCell in interface IController
      Parameters:
      row - The row (or y-coordinate) of the target cell.
      col - The column (or x-coordinate) of the target cell.
      Throws:
      InvalidCellException - if the given cell coordinates do not exist within the Sudoku grid.
      EmptyCellException - if the given cell is already empty.
      GivenCellException - if trying to clear a given cell.

    • hint

      public void hint() throws GridSolvedException
      Description copied from interface: IController
      Generates a new given cell in a random place on the Sudoku grid.

      If all cells are filled, but the grid has not yet been solved, the algorithm will replace a cell with an existing, but incorrect, entry with a given value.

      The algorithm will never place a hint in a cell in which the player has placed a value which is already correct.

      Specified by:
      hint in interface IController
      Throws:
      GridSolvedException - if the grid has already been solved: there are no more cells left to hint.

    • solve

      public void solve() throws GridSolvedException
      Description copied from interface: IController
      Completes the grid by filling in any blank cells, and replacing any that are incorrect.
      Specified by:
      solve in interface IController
      Throws:
      GridSolvedException - if the grid has already been solved.

    • reset

      public void reset() throws EmptyGridException
      Description copied from interface: IController
      Resets the grid to an empty state.

      Note that given/hint cells are not cleared.

      Specified by:
      reset in interface IController
      Throws:
      EmptyGridException - if the grid is already empty.

    • getGridState

      public ISudokuGrid getGridState()
      Description copied from interface: IController
      Returns the current state of the Sudoku grid.
      Specified by:
      getGridState in interface IController
      Returns:
      the current state of the Sudoku grid.
      See Also:
